case_name: Market-friendly (optimistic but reasonable) — beta held at 0.90
market_price_note: $450 is the round reference figure used in Planning Record §6 ('market trading around $450'); illustrative, not a dated quote.
purpose: A market-friendly Microsoft case: same frozen engine, only the input fixture changed. Built to sit beside the conservative headline fixture so the page can show both a sober and a defensible-central read of the same company. Engine, convergence, and shock logic were NOT modified.
assumptions: Extended from 7 to 10 for a slow, stable fade
Gradual expansion as higher-margin mix grows
Microsoft's historical beta, held fixed — not tuned
Natural result of CAPM at beta 0.90 (rf 4.3% / ERP 5.5% / cost-of-debt 3.8% / debt-weight 1.5%). Fully defensible, nothing reverse-solved.
note: Every CAPM input is held at its conventional value; beta is Microsoft's historical 0.90, not reverse-solved to hit a target. The only changes from the conservative headline fixture are the forecast paths, the 10-year horizon, and terminal growth 2.5% -> 3.0%. This is the more market-aligned of the two reads; the 7-year / 2.5%-g headline fixture is the more cautious one. Both are honest.
